observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct;
a) The Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer on
or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, an assessment roll
containing a list of all persons whose real properties had been newly assessed
or reassessed and the values of such properties.
b) The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Account a duly certified list
showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the
current year. The real property tax receivable and special education tax
receivable would be established not exceeding 1% of the assessed value to real
property appearing in the Real Property Tax Account Register or Taxpayer’s
index card.

2.7 In view of the above discussion, we recommended the LCE to instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort in the collections of the Account
Receivables to pave a way to increase revenue to the municipality’s coffers.

3.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
1) Property and/or Supply Division/Unit:
a) Segregate the PPE that serve Semi-Expendable Property.
a.1) Acquire prior to CY 2022 amounting from P 15,000.00 to
below P 50,000.00 in the custody of the Supply and/or
Property Division/Unit. The Property Cards would serve as Semi-
Expandable Property Card (SPCs) until later were issued pursuant
to Section 4.4 (b) of COA Circular No. 2022-004,
a.2) Acquire prior to CY 2022 amounting below P 15,000.00
already classified as semi-expandable property and still
at the custody of the Supply and/or Property Division/Unit. The
Stock Cards would serve us SPCs until later were issued in accordance
with Section 4.5 (a) of COA Circular No. 2022-004.
b) Prepare the Inventory Custodian Slip by the Property and/or
Supply Custodian.
c) With the ICS, prepare the RSPI in accordance with Section
4.7.7 Annex A.7 of the same Circular.
d) Submit the RSPI to the Accounting Division/Unit.
2) Accounting Division/Unit:
a) Do the following upon receipt of the RSPI:
a.1) Acquire prior to CY 2022 from the P 15,000.00 to below
P 50,000.00 previously classified as PPE in accordance with
Section 4.3 (iii) of same Circular.
a.1.1) The carrying amount would be
expensed/charged to Prior Period Adjustment
and Government Equipment.
a.1.2) Corresponding accumulated Depreciation and
accumulated Impairment loss would be covered
in the books of accounts.
a.2) Acquire prior to CY 2022 with amounts from P 15,000.00
to below P 50,000.00 previously classified as PPE which
were still in the custody of the Supply and/or Property
Division/Unit in accordance with Section 4.4 of the same
Circular.
a.2.1) Reclassify to the appropriate semi-expendable
property account. The Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards would serve as Semi-
Expandable Property Ledger Cards (SPLCs)
until issued.
a.3) Acquire prior to CY 2022 below P 15,000.00 which were
classified as semi-expendable property and still the
custody of the Supply and/or Property Division/Unit
pursuant to Section 4.5 (b) of the same Circular.
a.3.1) The Supplies Ledger Cards would serve as
SPLCs until issued.
b) Apply the change in the accounting policy in accordance with
Section 4.6 of the same Circular.

4.7 In view of the foregoing discussion, we recommended that the LCE
instruct the MPDC to submit the inventory of local roads together with
the reconciled road map and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the
subsidiary ledgers on the roads.

5.6 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust
the Loan payable as shown above.

1. We recommend to the Management the following:
1.1 Municipal Accountant
1.1.1 Adhere to Section 1 (2) of COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
1.1.2 Determine the components of Local Roads identified in the inventory.
1.1.3 Keep and maintain subsidiary records.
1.1.4 Prepare a schedule for the computation of depreciation.
1.1.5 Depreciate the
Note CY 2014 and 2015
1. Building of Market and Slaughter house
2. Office Equipment
3. Furniture and Fixture
1.2 Municipal Supply Officer.
1.2.1 Abide to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree no. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code the Philippines.
1.2.2 Submit Physical Inventory Report / Report of Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team.

2. We recommend to the Management the following:
2.1 Municipal Accountant
2.1.1 Adhere to Section 1 (2) of COA Circular No 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
2.1.2 Determine the components of Local Roads identified in the inventory.
2.1.3 Keepand maintain subsidiary records.
2.1.4 Prepare a schedule for the computation of depreciation.
2.1.5 Provide Depreciation for the Property, Plant and Equipment under the Trust Fund Account
2.2 Municipal Supply Officer
2.2.1 Abide to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
2.2.2 Submit Report of Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team.

3. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to reclassify Other Public Infrastructures Account to its appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts in accordance with Section 04 (n) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS for Local Government Units).

4. We recommend to the Management to instruct the following official and personnel to comply:
4.1 Local Chief Executive
4.1.1 Create an Inventory-Taking Committee, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
4.2 Municipal Accountant
4.2.1 To recompute the depreciations of the following Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), pursuant to Sections 260 and 262 (h and j) of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I.
4.2.1.1 General Fund
4.2.1.1.1 Road Networks
4.2.1.1.2 Water Supply
4.2.1.1.3 Bridge
4.2.1.1.4 Other Public Infrastructures
4.2.2 Trust Fund
4.2.2.1 Water Supply System
4.2.2.2 Office Building
4.2.1.2.3 Other Structures
4.2.1.2.4 Office Equipment
5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit it to the audit team, in accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 of Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for the Local Government Units, Volume I.

5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit it to the audit team, in accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 of Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for the Local Government Units, Volume I.

6. We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) the following:
6.1 Adhere to Section 2 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
6.2 Require the Municipal Accountant to submit early the Financial Statements to his office to give an ample time for review if there is/are any error/s, measures can still be made.
6.3 Check the Financial standing and Position of his agency.
6.4 Update the audit team on the payments made.

7. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant the following:
7.1 Municipal Treasurer
7.1.1 Abide to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
7.1.2 Prepare, update and submit the Cashbooks and its pertinent documents to avoid inconvenience at the time of cash examination.
7.1.3 Submit the Bond, Statement of Assets, Liabilities and Networth (SALN), Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and Report of Accountability for Accountable Form (RAAF).
7.2 Municipal Accountant
7.2.1 Abide to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
7.2.2 Prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
7.2.3 Submit to the Audit Team the BRS together with the Bank Statements, Deposit Slips, Debit and Credit Memos and the Subsidiary Ledger of the Municipal Treasurer.

8. We recommend to the Management the following:
8.1 Local Chief Executive
8.1.1 To apply the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking in accordance with Section 3.0 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and 2020.
8.1.2 To create the Inventory Committee composing of the Accounting, Property Divisions/Units and Internal Audit Service/Unit for the purpose of cleansing the PPE Account in accordance with Section 5.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.2 Inventory Committee
8.2.1 To prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) in accordance with Section 5.9 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.2 .2 To conduct the Physical Inventory taking pursuant to Section 5.3 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.2.3 To prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in pursuant of Section 6.2.13 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3. Property Unit
8.3.1 To ensure that the recording are updated pursuant to Section 6.3.1(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3.2 To prepare Property Cards for items found at station, in accordance with Section 6.3.1(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3.3 To prepare list of non existing/missing PPEs in accordance with Section 6.3.1(d) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3.4 To prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) in accordance with Section 6.3.1(h) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.3.5 To reconcile with the Accounting Unit in accordance with Section 6.3.1(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.4 Accounting Unit
8.4.1 To take necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs found at station and prepare corresponding Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards based on the List of PPEs found at Station in accordance with Section 6.3.2(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.4.2 To take up necessary accounting entries to recognize loss of PPE and to set up corresponding receivables from concerned accountable officer pursuant to Section 6.3.2(b) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.4.3 To reconcile with the Property Unit, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.4.4 To take up necessary accounting entries to write-off/drop from the books of accounts the remaining balance as of December 31, 2021, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(e) of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
8.4.5 To ensure the total balance of PPELCs/SLs totally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledgers, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8.5 Internal Audit Service/Unit
8.5.1 To witness the conduct of the inventory in accordance with Section 5.5 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

9. In view of the foregoing, we recommend that the Management shall
9.1 Strictly enforce the provision of Section 10 and 48 of RA 9184 and Item 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 by strictly procuring goods, consulting services and infrastructure of the Municipality of Barira through competitive bidding or alternative modes of procurement; and
9.2 Stop the practice of procurement through re-imbursement basis to be granted to all Municipal Officials and Employees. Hence, all transactions shall be approved with required documentary requirements in compliance top said Circular and will be directly paid to suppliers.

10. We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the above personnel mentioned below the following:
10.1 Supply Officer
10.1 Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Water Vehicle.
10.2 Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption (MFC).
10.3 Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTT) and Monthly Fuel Consumption.
10.2 Drivers
10.2.1 To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
10.2.2 To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels (MROT) where he summarizes in chronological order his trip for the month.
10.2.3 To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.
10.3 Chief Accountant
10.3.1 To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the Drivers to the Auditor.
11. We recommend the management to adopt the uniform property identification system, pursuant to Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

12. We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the required documents for inspection by the Regional Technical Service Office (RTSO), Commission on Audit-BARMM in accordance with Sections 18 and 22 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

4. We recommend to the management to instruct the official and date when the personnel to comply:
4.1 Local Chief Executive
4.1.1 Create an Inventory-Taking Committee, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
4.2 Municipal Accountant
4.2.1 To recompute the depreciations of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), pursuant to Sections 260 and 262 (h and j) of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I.
4.2.1.1 General Fund
4.2.1.1.1 Road Networks
4.2.1.1.2 Water Supply
4.2.1.1.3 Bridge
4.2.1.1.4 Other Public Infrastructures
4.2.2 Trust Fund
4.2.2.1 Water Supply System
4.2.2.2 Office Building
4.2.1.2.3 Other Structures
4.2.1.2.4 Office Equipment
observations.reason: Problem to identity and no constructed.

7.1 Adhere to Section 2 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
7.2 Require the Municipal Accountant to submit early the Financial Statements to his office to give an ample time for review if there is/are any error/s, measures can still be made.
7.3 Check the Financial standing and Position of his agency.
7.4 Update the audit team on the payments made.
observations.reason: Still in the process of identify certain obligations.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant the following:

a) Municipal Treasurer
a.1 Abide to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D) No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines.
a.2 Prepare, update and submit the Cashbooks and its pertinent
documents to avoid inconvenience at the time of cash
examination.
a.3 Submit the Bond, Statement of Assets, Liabilities and Networth
(SALN), Report of Collections and Reports (RCD) and Report
of Accountability for Accountable Form (RAAF).

b) Municipal Accountant
b.1 Abide to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996.
b.2 Prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS).
b.3 Submit to the Audit Team the BRS together with the Bank
Statements, Deposit Slips, Debit and Credit Memos and the
Subsidiary Ledger of the Municipal Treasurer.

2.12 We recommend to the management the following:

a) Local Chief Executive
a.1 To apply the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking, in
accordance with Section 3.0 of the Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
a.2 To create the Inventory Committee composing of the
Accounting, Property Divisions/Units and Internal Audit
Service/Unit for the purpose of cleansing the PPE Account, in
accordance with Section 5.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020.

b) Inventory Committee
b.1 To prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), in accordance
with Section 5.9 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.
b.2 To conduct the Physical Inventory taking, pursuant to Section
5.3 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020.
b.3 To prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE), in pursuant of Section 6.2.13 of
COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

c) Property Unit
c.1 To ensure that the recording are updated, pursuant to Section
6.3.1(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.
c.2 To prepare Property Cards for items found at station, in
accordance with Section 6.3.1(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-
006 dated January 31, 2020.
c.3 To prepare list of non existing/missing PPEs, in accordance
with Section 6.3.1(d) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.
c.4 To prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property (IIRUP), in accordance with Section 6.3.1(h) of the
COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
c.5 To reconcile with the Accounting Unit, in accordance with
Section 6.3.1(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.

d) Accounting Unit
d.1 To take necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs found
at station and prepare corresponding Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards based on the List of PPEs found at
Station, in accordance with Section 6.3.2(a) of the COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
d.2 To take up necessary accounting entries to recognize loss of
PPE and to set up corresponding receivables from concerned
accountable officer, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(b) of the COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
d.3 To reconcile with the Property Unit, pursuant to Section
6.3.2(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.
d.4 To take up necessary accounting entries to write-off/drop from
the books of accounts the remaining balance as of December
31, 2021, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(e) of COA Circular No.
2020-006.
d.5 To ensure the total balance of PPELCs/SLs totally with the
balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledgers,
pursuant to Section 6.3.2(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020.

e) Internal Audit Service/Unit
e.1 To witness the conduct of the inventory, in accordance with
Section 5.5 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January
31, 2020.

3.11 In view of the foregoing, we recommend that the Management shall
a.1 Strictly enforce the provision of Section 10 and 48 of RA 9184 and
Item 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 by strictly procuring goods,
consulting services and infrastructure of the Municipality of Barira
through competitive bidding or alternative modes of procurement; and
a.2 Stop the practice of procurement through re-imbursement basis to
be granted to all Municipal Officials and Employees. Hence, all
transactions shall be approved with required documentary
requirements in compliance top said Circular and will be directly
paid to suppliers.

4.10 We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the above
personnel mentioned below the following:

a) Supply Officer
a.1 Abide to Section D.7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel
Consumption of Government Water Vehicle.
a.2 Prepare the Monthly Fuel Consumption (MFC).
a.3 Submit to the Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTT) and
Monthly Fuel Consumption.

b) Drivers
b.1 To adhere to Section D.6 of the above Manual.
b.2 To prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels (MROT)
where he summarizes in chronological order his trip for the
month.
b.4 To submit the above report to the Chief Accountant.

c) Chief Accountant
c.1 To submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels of the Drivers
to the Auditor.

5.6 We recommend the management to adopt the uniform property
identification system, pursuant to Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of the Commission
on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

6.5 We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the required
documents for inspection by the Regional Technical Service Office
(RTSO), Commission on Audit-BARMM in accordance with Sections 18
and 22 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

1. We recommend to the management the following:
1.1.1 Adhere to Section 1 (2) of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
1.1.2 Determine the components of Local Roads identified in the inventory.
1.1.3 Keep and maintain subsidiary records.
1.1.4 Prepare a schedule for the computation of depreciation.
1.1.5 Depreciate the Road Networks with the Construction Cost of ₱ 229,685,579.61.
1.2 Municipal Supply Officer
1.2.1 Abide to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree no. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
1.2.2 Submit Physical Inventory Report / Report of Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to reclassify Other Public Infrastructures Account to its appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts in accordance with Section 04 (n) of the Manual of the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS for Local Government Units).

observations.recommendation:
5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant the following:
5.1 Municipal Assessor
5.1 Conduct physical surveys to ascertain all properties are appropriately included in the assessment rolls, in accordance with Section 42 paragraph (b) (1).
5.1.2 Submit every semester a report to the LCE and Sanggunian Bayan of all assessments for any cancellation and changes.
5.1.3 Submit the list of assessment roll to the Municipal Accountant, in accordance with Section 472 paragraph (b) (7) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
5.2 Municipal Accountant
5.2.2 To set up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Property Tax in the books of accounts, pursuant to Section 474 paragraph (b) (13) R.A. No. 7160.

8. We recommend to the Accountable Officer to comply the following:
8.1 Abide with Section 5.10.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
8.2 Submit the deficient supporting documents such as Purchase Request, Purchase Order and Acceptance and Inspection Report.

9. We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to do the following:
9.1 Adhere to Section 4 (6) of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
9.2 Check and examine the appropriateness of documents on every financial transactions of the said Local Government Unit.
9.3 Check the completeness of supporting documents per transactions.
9.4 With the assurance of the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents on every transactions, sign in Box B of the Disbursement Vouchers to consummate the financial transactions.

10. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle the Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

11. We recommend to the management to implement the audit recommendations in the Status of Implementation of Prior Years’ Recommendations (SIPYR) for CY 2020 specifically on observation nos. 2,3,4 and 5 as partially implemented, pursuant to Section 3 (II) (D), Section 5 (V) (16) and Appendix 5-4 of the Financial Audit Manual (FAM).

53. We commend the Municipal Government of Barira, Maguindanmao for its effort in
the implementation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund, in
accordance with Section 21 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 10121, otherwise known as an
act strengthening the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management System,
providing for the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Framework and
Institutionalizing the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan.

61. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Assessor and Municipal Accountant the following:
61.1 Municipal Assessor
61.1.1 Conduct physical surveys to ascertain all properties are
appropriately included in the assessment rolls, in accordance with
Section 42 paragraph (b) (1).
61.1.2 Submit every semester a report to the LCE and Sanggunian
Bayan of all assessments for any cancellation and changes.
61.1.3 Submit the list of assessment roll to the Municipal
Accountant, in accordance with Section 472 paragraph (b) (7) of the
Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local
Government Code of 1991.
61.2 Municipal Accountant
61.2.2 To set up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Property Tax
in the books of accounts, pursuant to Section 474 paragraph (b)
(13) R.A. No. 7160.

71. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Inventory
Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit it to the audit team, in accordance with Section
156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992
and Section 124 of Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS)
for the Local Government Units, Volume I.

80. We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) the following:
80.1 Adhere to Section 2 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
80.2 Require the Municipal Accountant to submit early the Financial
Statements to his office to give an ample time for review if there is/are any
error/s, measures can still be made.
80.3 Check the Financial standing and Position of his agency.
80.4 Update the audit team on the payments made.

85. We recommend to the Accountable Officer to comply the following:
85.1 Abide with Section 5.10.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular
No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
85.2 Submit the deficient supporting documents such as Purchase Request,
Purchase Order and Acceptance and Inspection Report.

101. We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to do the following:
101.1 Adhere to Section 4 (6) of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
101.2 Check and examine the appropriateness of documents on every
financial transactions of the said Local Government Unit.
101.3 Check the completeness of supporting documents per transactions.
101.4 With the assurance of the completeness and propriety of the
supporting documents on every transactions, sign in Box B of the
Disbursement Vouchers to consummate the financial transactions.

106. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel
concerned to settle the Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

113. We recommend to the management to implement the audit recommendations
in the Status of Implementation of Prior Years Recommendations (SIPYR) for CY
2020 specifically on observation nos. 2,3,4 and 5 as partially implemented, pursuant
to Section 3 (II) (D), Section 5 (V) (16) and Appendix 5-4 of the Financial Audit
Manual (FAM).

2. Recommendation
52. We recommend to the management to instruct the following official and
personnel to comply:

Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units
(LGUs), Volume I.
52.2 Municipal Accountant

Plant and Equipment (PPE), pursuant to Sections 260 and
262 (h and j) of the Government Accounting Manual
(GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I.

Recommendation
61. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should:
61.1 Abide to Sections 2 and 111 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines
and Appendix 43 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for
Local Government Units, Volume II.
61.2 Maintain an Index of Payment (IP) on the disbursements made.
61.3 Compute accurately the remittance to the Philippine Health Insurance
Corporation (PHIC).
61.4 Seek the assistance of other personnel in the counter checking the
computation of the remittances.

67. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct personnel
concerned to settle the Notice of Suspension, in accordance with Section 9.4 of the

Recommendation
73. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) the following:
73.1 Adhere to Section 21 of the R.A No. 10121, otherwise as an Act
Strengthening the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction Management
System, providing for the National Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Plan appropriating fund thereof and other purposes.
73.2 Review the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and
Budget for the implementation of the six (6) Programs, Projects and
Activities (PPAs).
73.3 Advice the LCE to include these projects in the ensuing years.
73.4 Monitor said PPAs for its proper implementation.

80. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal
Accountant to abide Section 114 (1) of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and maintain
the prescribed General Ledgers forms.

3. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to submit the Solid Waste Management Plan for CY 2017, in accordance with Section 16 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of the Philippines.

4. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant the following: 4.1 Municipal Treasurer 4.1.1 To adhere to 100 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines. 4.1.2 To submit to the team the cash books. 4.2 Municipal Accountant 4.2.1 To abide to Section 100 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines. 4.2.2 To submit the bank reconciliation statements together with the bank statements, paid checks, deposit slip, credit and debit memos.

5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to: 5.1 Municipal Treasurer 5.1.1 Abide to Section 6 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-385 dated July 3, 1992. 5.1.2 Conduct tax campaign / drive on the Real Property Tax. 5.1.3 Exert extra effort to collect the real property tax. 5.2 Municipal Accountant 5.2.1 Adhere to Section 272 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991. 5.2.2 Transfer the collected 1% of the Real Property Tax from the General Fund to the Special Education Fund.

7. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to reclassify Other Public Infrastructures Account to its appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts in accordance with Section 04 (n) of the Manual of the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS for Local Government Units).

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to submit the Solid Waste Management Plan for CY 2018, in accordance with Section 16 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of the Philippines.

9. We recommend to the management to strengthen their Gender and Development Focal Point System (GFPS) to pursue the gender mainstreaming of GAD in the LGUs activities and programs. The GFPS will be the one in charge in the monitoring of the implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget of the LGU. Also, the management should submit their GAD Plan and Budget to Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) and PPDO will be the one to submit to the DILG-ARMM for approval and endorsement, in accordance with Section 36 (a) of Republic Act (RA) 9710 and Section 5 (2) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.
10. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

53. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal
Accountant to withhold applicable taxes from all payments made to suppliers and
creditors in accordance with Section a and b of the National Internal Revenue Code
of the Philippines and Taxes deducted and withheld under Section 57 by
withholding agents shall be covered by a return and paid to, except in cases where
the Commissioner otherwise permits, an authorized agent bank, Revenue District
Officer, Collection Agent, or duly authorized Treasurer of the city or municipality
where the withholding agent has his legal residence or principal place of business, or
where the withholding agent is a corporation, where the principal office is located.
54. The taxes deducted and withheld by the withholding agent shall be held as a
special fund in trust for the government until paid to the collecting officers (Section
58 of RA 8424).

Auditing Code of the Philippines.

Management Plan for CY 2017, in accordance with Section 16 of the Republic Act
(RA) No. 7160, otherwise known on the Local Government Code of the Philippines.
It is recommended that the hiring of Consultants and Casual Employees shall be based on the necessity and urgency of the job. Refrain from hiring of Casuals employees of continued services.

We reiterate our recommendation to cause the immediate physical inventory taking on all the Properties, Plant and Equipment and recognize depreciation expense for the proper matching of cost and expense, in compliance with the provisions of the NGAS.

Direct the General Service Office (GSO) to maintain the needed documents in the supporting determination of the propriety of issuance of gasoline to enable the management to monitor as well as regulate their usage. The Accounting unit should review transaction documents to ensure completeness and validity of supporting documents before payments made. Strictly observe the guidelines in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 prescribing the use of Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Motor Vehicles.

We recommend that the municipality shall require the school and the grantee to strictly adhere to the provisions of CHED Memorandum Order No.15 s. 2009 in order to ascertain the validity of grants given.

We recommend that the Municipal accountant shall recognized accounting entries prescribe in COA Circular No. 96-003 dated February 27, 1996 in order to pin point accountabilities in handling government fund released to NGOs/POs.

We recommend that the municipality shall require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of perfected contracts and its supporting documents within the period prescribed by law to facilitate timely review and evaluation. And also officials responsible for the execution of contracts shall furnish copies of all contracts to COA.

The Municipality shall follow strictly the guidelines set forth in DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.1 s. 2005 dated September 20, 2005 and focus to developmental expenditures and also invest a substantial portion of the fund in investment and capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Adhere to the guidelines set forth of the DILG and DBM guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality shall require the school and the grantee to strictly adhere to the provisions of CHED Memorandum Order No. 15, s. 2009 in order to ascertain the validity of grants given.

We recommend that the Municipal accountant shall recognized accounting entries prescribe in COA Circular No. 96-003 dated February 27, 1996 in order to pin point accountabilities in handling government fund released to NGOs/POs.

We recommend that the municipality shall require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of perfected contracts and its supporting documents within the period prescribed by law to facilitate timely review and evaluation. And also officials responsible for the activities that necessitate the execution of contracts shall furnish copies of all contracts to COA.

The Municipality shall follow strictly the guidelines set forth in DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2005 dated September 20, 2005 and focus to developmental expenditures projects and also invest a substantial portion of the fund in investment and capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Adhere to the provisions of the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the personnel concerned to submit the listing of real property tax declarants, amount, date due and subsidiary ledger, pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the State Audit Code of the Philippines.
observations.reason: On-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to depreciate two (2) properties in the Trust Fund consisting of the Office Building with      ₱ 6,947,951.09 and Other Structure amounting to                ₱ 1,488,975.00 and set up Salvage Value in the General Fund for the four (4) properties with Gross Amount of                  ₱ 2,550,265.00, pursuant to Section 59 of PPSAS 17-Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction: Management complied some of the requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to abide to Section 111 of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and submit the books of accounts.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to submit the report on the Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund, pursuant to Section 39 of the Republic Act No.10651, otherwise known as the FY 2015 General Appropriation Act.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports, paid disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents, pursuant to paragraph 7.3 of COA Circular No.94-006 dated February 17, 1994, as amended and Section 122 of Presidential Decree No.1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
